content: Current LLM self-reflection mechanisms that integrate multiple inconsistent problem-solving perspectives can be embedded into the Self-Refine iterative workflow to create a hybrid self-improvement system that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of requiring no extra training or supervised data, while resolving Self-Refine's blind spot of limited feedback diversity from single-perspective self-evaluation. This hybrid framework can outperform both standalone Self-Refine and single-step multiple-perspective reflection across reasoning, code generation, and open-ended text generation tasks.
